per_question.expected_preview: WeakEntity<T> in Zed (GPUI framework) is a weak reference to an entity that doesn't prevent it from
per_question.answer_preview: WeakEntity<T> prevents cycles by converting Entity<T> to a weak reference. If entity is dropped before the future completes, upgrade() returns None and the future can be cancelled gracefully.

per_question.expected_preview: In Zed, workspace.register_action() connects keybindings to handler functions. Pattern seen in vim/s

per_question.expected_preview: Task::ready() creates an immediately-resolved Task<T> without spawning on executor. Found in vim/com
per_question.answer_preview: Task::ready() creates an immediately-ready task, useful for converting synchronous operations to async. Used when you need to return a Task but don't have async work. More efficient than spawning.

per_question.expected_preview: Anyhow's context methods add descriptive information to errors as they propagate. Found throughout Z
per_question.answer_preview: .context(msg) computes error context eagerly. .with_context(|| msg) computes lazily only if error occurs. Use with_context() for expensive context computation.

per_question.expected_preview: The bail! macro in Zed provides early error returns. Found in lmstudio.rs, vim/command.rs, agent_ui
per_question.answer_preview: bail!(msg) expands to 'return Err(anyhow!(msg))'. It returns immediately on error. Differs from Err(anyhow!(msg)) which requires explicit return statement.

per_question.expected_preview: In crates/client/src/telemetry.rs (lines 82-90), Zed implements event integrity verification using Z

per_question.expected_preview: BackgroundExecutor in GPUI is the runtime for async tasks. When cx.spawn() is called, it creates a T
per_question.answer_preview: BackgroundExecutor is managed by GPUI framework. cx.spawn() captures executor reference and schedules closure on it. Executor runs tasks in background thread pool to avoid blocking UI.

per_question.expected_preview: In crates/settings/src/keymap_file.rs (lines 69-75), KeymapSection has a use_key_equivalents boolean
per_question.answer_preview: use_key_equivalents field controls whether to map keys by physical position (QWERTY) or by key code. Non-QWERTY keyboard support incomplete - only Mac fully supported.

per_question.expected_preview: util::defer() (seen in peer.rs line 156) implements the defer pattern - run code when scope exits re
per_question.answer_preview: util::defer() creates a guard that runs cleanup code when dropped (RAII pattern). In peer.rs connection handling, defer() ensures resources cleaned up even if connection fails partway through setup.

per_question.expected_preview: The buffer will panic with an assertion failure. The code explicitly asserts that transaction_depth
per_question.answer_preview: Calling end_transaction() when transaction_depth == 0 panics. It's a critical assertion to catch logic errors where transactions are unbalanced.

per_question.expected_preview: All three operations require that transaction_depth equals 0. The code enforces this with `assert_eq
per_question.answer_preview: Constraint: transaction_depth must be > 0 for push_transaction, pop_undo, pop_redo. These operations can only occur inside a transaction, enforced by assertion.

per_question.expected_preview: The has_conflict flag is set to true (line 1629 in buffer.rs). When this flag is true, the buffer is
per_question.answer_preview: When reload detects concurrent edits, the 'conflict' flag is set. This prevents auto-save and prompts user to resolve the conflict, preventing data loss.

per_question.expected_preview: The invariant is that start <= end always (selections are stored in canonical order). The set_head m
per_question.answer_preview: Selection invariant: start <= end always holds. set_head() reorders start/end if needed, maintaining invariant. Start is the anchor, end is where head is.

per_question.expected_preview: An edit is considered undone if its undo_count is odd (modulo 2 equals 1), as seen in line 69 of und
per_question.answer_preview: An edit is 'undone' if its ID appears in the undo_stack but not the redo_stack. UndoMap tracks this with two concurrent sets.

per_question.expected_preview: The operation is deferred and stored in the deferred_ops OperationQueue. The can_apply_op method (li
per_question.answer_preview: Deferred operations are queued when buffer can't resolve anchors yet (remote operations arrive before their dependencies). They're applied retroactively once dependencies are available.

per_question.expected_preview: Merged operations have their timestamps stored in the merged_operations vector (line 1377 in buffer.
per_question.answer_preview: In merge_into_base, operations are marked as merged and tracked. This is important to avoid re-applying them when syncing back to base branch.

per_question.expected_preview: always_deny takes absolute precedence over always_allow. According to the precedence documentation i
per_question.answer_preview: Tool permissions precedence: always_deny wins over always_allow. If both match, deny takes precedence for security.

per_question.expected_preview: If any regex patterns in a tool's rules failed to compile (stored in invalid_patterns), the tool is
per_question.answer_preview: Invalid regex patterns in tool permissions cause that pattern to be treated as non-matching. This prevents security bypasses from regex injection.

per_question.expected_preview: It does NOT override always_deny patterns. Even with always_allow_tool_actions=true, if an always_de
per_question.answer_preview: always_allow_tool_actions does NOT override if: (1) tool_deny_patterns match, (2) tool is marked as always_deny in settings. It respects explicit denials.

per_question.expected_preview: The was_dirty_before_starting_transaction field is set only on the outermost transaction (when trans
per_question.answer_preview: Nested transaction constraint: was_dirty_before_starting_transaction must be set only at the outermost transaction level, not in nested transactions.

per_question.expected_preview: The disjoint field contains non-overlapping selections (line 31-32 in selections_collection.rs), whi
per_question.answer_preview: SelectionsCollection maintains invariant: disjoint selections never overlap. pending selections are being edited. Guarantee: all non-pending selections are disjoint.

per_question.expected_preview: Fragment IDs must be strictly increasing (line 1773 in text.rs): `assert!(fragment.id > prev_fragmen
per_question.answer_preview: BufferSnapshot::check_invariants ensures all fragment IDs are unique and sorted. This is critical because fragment IDs are used for deduplication in CRDT operations.

per_question.expected_preview: The diff is adjusted to account for intervening edits, and conflicting parts are discarded (line 227
per_question.answer_preview: If apply_diff uses an old version, it rebases the diff on current state first. This ensures correctness even if buffer has been edited since diff was computed.

per_question.expected_preview: When capability is Capability::ReadOnly (defined at line 87 in buffer.rs), the buffer cannot be edit
per_question.answer_preview: ReadOnly capability prevents all text edits. Attempts to edit fail with PermissionDenied error. Read operations still work normally.

per_question.expected_preview: The operation is ignored/rejected. Lines 3007-3011 in buffer.rs check if the stored selection set ha
per_question.answer_preview: If UpdateSelections arrives with older lamport_timestamp, it's ignored. This prevents old selections from overwriting newer ones due to network delays.

per_question.expected_preview: push_empty_transaction creates a parent transaction without clearing the redo stack (lines 2448-2460
per_question.answer_preview: push_empty_transaction() creates a transaction that emits undo/redo events even if no edits occur. Caller is responsible for ensuring this is semantically meaningful.

per_question.expected_preview: The group_interval field (default 300ms for production, 0 for tests per lines 196-200 in text.rs) de
per_question.answer_preview: History maintains time window constraint: transactions are only grouped if timestamps are within 500ms (configurable). Older transactions aren't automatically grouped with new ones.

per_question.expected_preview: The buffer validates that the anchor's buffer_id matches the snapshot's remote_id (lines 2306-2316 i
per_question.answer_preview: Before resolving an Anchor, Buffer::version must match the Anchor's version. If mismatch, anchor is stale and resolution fails. This prevents anchors from pointing to wrong positions.

per_question.expected_preview: The saved_version is updated in did_save (line 1529) and reload operations (line 1620-1621). When up
per_question.answer_preview: saved_version is updated only when buffer is marked as saved. At same time, the edit_count is cleared (reset to 0), indicating no unsaved changes.
